2: And my calamity laid in the balances together!
3: Therefore my words are swallowed up.
4: The terrors of God do set themselves in array against me.
5: Or loweth the ox over his fodder?
6: Or is there any any taste in the white of an egg?
7: Are Are as my sorrowful meat.
8: And that God would grant me me the thing that I long for!
9: That he would let loose his hand, and cut me off!
10: For I have not concealed the words of the Holy One.
11: And what is is mine end, that I should prolong my life?
12: Or is is my flesh of brass?
13: And is wisdom driven quite from me?
14: But he forsaketh the fear of the Almighty.
15: And And as the stream of brooks they pass away;
16: And And wherein the snow is hid:
17: When it is hot, they are consumed out of their place.
18: They go to nothing, and perish.
19: The companies of Sheba waited for them.
20: They came thither, and were ashamed.
21: Ye see my my casting down, and are afraid.
22: Or, Give a reward for me of your substance?
23: Or, Redeem me from the hand of the mighty?
24: And cause me to understand wherein I have erred.
25: But what doth your arguing reprove?
26: And the speeches of one that is desperate, which are which are as wind?
27: And ye dig a pit a pit for your friend.
28: For it is it is evident unto you if I lie.
29: Yea, return again, my righteousness is is in it.
30: Cannot my taste discern perverse things?
